page: Microsoft Azure E4as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Easv7 server family. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU.

This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external network-attached disks, though the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this virtual machine.

The high memory density relative to the vCPU count makes this instance cost-efficient for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity without demanding high compute core counts. However, the lack of local storage and hardware acceleration presents a tradeoff for applications requiring high-speed local scratch space or parallelized graphics processing. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E4as_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Easv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated graphics processing units, though it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments. The high memory density makes this server a cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require proportional scaling of compute resources. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring reliance on network-attached storage. This virtual machine is suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise application hosting.
